They are competing in the [[2026 Super League season]] and the [[2026 Challenge Cup]]. ==Preseason friendlies== Wigan's preseason game against [[Hull FC]] was [[Liam Marshall]]'s testimonial match.{{Cite news|last=Darbyshire|first=Drew|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-pre-season-friendly-confirmed-in-liam-marshalls-testimonial-match-5393717|title=Wigan Warriors pre-season friendly confirmed in Liam Marshall's testimonial match |date=7 November 2025|work=Wigan Today|access-date=27 November 2025}} {| class="wikitable defaultleft col4center col7center col10right col12center" style="font-size:85%;" !Date and time!!Versus!!H/A!!Venue!!Result!!Score!!Tries!!Goals!!Attendance!!TV!!Report |- style="background:#ffdddd;" | 10 January; 13:00 | {{leagueicon|North wales|16}} [[North Wales Crusaders]] | A | [[Eirias Stadium]] | L | 22–34 | [[Tiaki Chan|Chan]], Wadsworth, [[Dayon Sambou|Sambou]], [[Noah Hodkinson|Hodkinson]] | Lowe (2/3) | 1,509 | Not televised |https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2026/01/10/warriors-beaten-by-crusaders-in-pre-season-opener/{{Dead link | date=May 2026 | fix-attempted=yes}} |- style="background:#ddffdd;" | 1 February; 15:00 | {{leagueicon|Hull fc|16}} [[Hull FC]] | H | [[Brick Community Stadium]] | W | 22–18 | [[Liam Marshall|Marshall]], [[Junior Nsemba|Nsemba]], [[Jai Field|Field]], [[Jack Farrimond|Farrimond]] | [[Adam Keighran|Keighran]] (2/3), [[Jack Farrimond|Farrimond]] (1/1) | 4,511 | Not televised |https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2026/02/01/warriors-beat-hull-fc-in-liam-marshall-testimonial/{{Dead link | date=May 2026 | fix-attempted=yes}} |} ==Super League== {{main|2026 Super League season}} From 2026, the [[Super League]] expanded to 14 teams, with [[Toulouse Olympique]] and [[York Knights]] being promoted from the [[RFL Championship]] via the [[IMG (company)|IMG]] [[IMG Grading for the British Rugby Football League|Grading system]].{{cite news |last1=Freeman |first1=Jay |title=York and Toulouse to play in expanded Super League |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cly24zvxw3no |access-date=16 December 2025 |publisher=BBC Sport |date=17 October 2025}} At a meeting of all twelve Super League teams at the [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]] on 28 July 2025 to vote on the expansion proposal, Wigan Warriors were the only club to abstain from voting.{{cite news |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |title=The three clubs who did not vote for Super League expansion revealed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/the-three-clubs-who-were-against-super-league-expansion-revealed |access-date=16 December 2025 |publisher=Love Rugby League |date=28 July 2025}} Wigan's 2026 Super League fixtures were announced on 27 November 2025,{{Cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12040/13475856/super-league-2026-fixtures-full-regular-season-fixture-list-plus-play-off-and-grand-final-dates|title=Super League 2026 fixtures: Full regular season fixture list, plus play-off and Grand Final dates |work=Sky Sports |date=27 November 2025 |access-date=28 November 2025}} with the opening round fixtures confirmed the day prior.{{Cite news|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/super-league-2026-round-one-fixtures-revealed|title=First BBC broadcast pick of new Super League season revealed in Round 1 schedule |first=Ben|last=Olawumi|date=26 November 2025|work=Love Rugby League|access-date=27 November 2025}} After speculation regarding its inclusion in the 2026 calendar, Wigan's [[Magic Weekend]] fixture was confirmed on 25 November; with the event taking place at the [[Bramley-Moore Dock Stadium]] in [[Liverpool]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c8dymy8p9gpo|title=Hill Dickinson Stadium to host 2026 Magic Weekend |date=25 November 2025|work=BBC Sport|access-date=27 November 2025}} Wigan's away fixture against [[Catalans Dragons]] was moved to [[Paris]] by the hosts in 2025 to celebrate 20 years of the club competing in [[Super League]], in addition to also marking 30 years of French clubs competing in the [[British rugby league system]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c9qxqz3ge1po|title=Catalans to face Wigan in Paris during 2026 season |date=25 July 2025|work=BBC Sport|access-date=27 November 2025}}{{Cite news|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/13401872/super-league-catalans-dragons-to-face-wigan-warriors-in-paris-in-2026-to-mark-20th-anniversary|title=Super League: Catalans Dragons to face Wigan Warriors in Paris in 2026 to mark 20th anniversary|work=Sky Sports|access-date=27 November 2025}} The match will be played as a double-header with the [[Super XIII 2025–2026]] Grand Final.{{Cite news|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wigan-warriors-catalans-dragons-paris-double-header-super-xiii|title=Wigan’s historic Paris trip to become historic double-header with second game on bill|work=Love Rugby League|date=21 December 2025|access-date=23 December 2025}} The club faced criticism for their round 12 squad section against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], naming 10 uncapped players in the match day squad a week ahead of the [[2026 Challenge Cup final|Challenge Cup final]]. The club quickly received backlash from its own fans and neutrals, with fans and journalists making similarities to [[Salford Red Devils]]'s round 1 squad in [[2025 Super League season|2025]] which they received a two point deduction for.https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/13545891/wigan-rest-star-names-for-hull-kr-clash-on-thursday-ahead-of-challenge-cup-final-nine-days-laterhttps://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cd0pkr10d2go The matter was investigated by the [[Rugby Football League]] who determined there was no breech of operational rules.https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/wigan-warriors-rfl-release-comment-1845927 ===Fixtures=== {| class="wikitable defaultleft col4center col7center col10right col12center" style="font-size:85%;" !Date and time!!Round!!Versus!!H/A!!Venue!!Result!!Score!!Tries!!Goals!!Attendance!!TV{{efn|Ahead of 2024, [[Sky Sports]] announced they would be broadcasting all Super League matches over the next three seasons.{{Cite news |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12984274/sky-sports-to-show-every-super-league-match-live-as-part-of-historic-new-three-year-tv-rights-deal |title=Sky Sports to show every Super League match live as part of historic new three-year TV rights deal|work=Sky Sports|access-date=7 September 2024}} Exact TV channels can be sourced by using [https://web.archive.org/ WayBack Machine] on [https://www.rugbyleagueontv.com/ RugbyLeagueOnTV].
''NB'' Not all games were broadcast on Sky Sports TV (advertised as "Sky Sports Exclusives"), the remainder were broadcast using Sky Sports+, their on demand services accessible online or via red button. These games were also made available on Super League+, and a select few on the [[BBC]].{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68229917 |title=Super League: BBC to show live games for first time after signing three-year broadcast deal|date=7 February 2024|work=BBC Sport|access-date=7 September 2024}} Further, games on Main Event or [[Sky One]] were also broadcast on Action.}}!!Pos.!!Report |- style="background:#ddffdd;" | 15 February;
